item.title: Visiting Italy: Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II
item.description: The Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II is a covered arcade situated on the northern side of the Piazza del Duomo in Milan, connecting to the Piazza della Scala. Named after King Victor Emmanuel II (It. Vittorio Emanuele II), the first king of united Italy, it was built by G. Mengoni between 1865 and 1878. The street is covered over by an arching glass and steel roof, thus giving it a claim to be the world ’s first

item.title: Visiting Italy: Duomo di Milano
item.description: The Duomo di Milano is one of the most famous buildings in Europe. It is the second largest Roman Catholic cathedral: only the cathedral of Seville is larger (as is Saint Peter ’s Basilica in Rome, which is not a cathedral). It is 157 meters long 40,000 people can fit comfortably within. The main spire is 109 meters high. The great windows of the choir were reputed to be the largest in the world.
